points.text: Tune Therapeutics reported Phase I data for TUNE-401, an epigenome editing therapy for chronic hepatitis B, achieving 95–100% durable silencing of HBV biomarkers in all treated patients.
Tune Therapeutics — TUNE-401 — Chronic Hepatitis B — Phase I trial demonstrated 95–100% durable repression of four key HBV biomarkers via epigenome editing targeting cccDNA and intDNA reservoirs.
Tune Therapeutics — Epigenome editing platform commercialization — TUNE-401 — Phase I data presented at EASL Congress showing proof of concept for durable gene silencing in humans.

TUNE-401 — Mechanism: Epigenome editing adding repressive marks to HBV cccDNA/intDNA — Phase: 1 — Population: Chronic Hepatitis B patients — Results: 95–100% durable silencing of four key HBV biomarkers across all treated subjects.
